en: You are tired, aren't you?
You aren't tired, are you?

tr: Ayşe yılbaşı partisi için heyecanlı, di mi?
en: Ayşe is excited for the New Year's party, isn't she?
You are here in the afternoon, aren't you?
The meeting is at 3 o'clock, isn't it?
It is rainy, isn't it?

tr: Ankara'da hava çok soğuk, değil mi?
en: It is very cold in Ankara, isn't it?
You can do this, can't you?
You can't do this, can you?
You can't walk, can you?
You can't speak English, can you?
You will go to Ankara, won't you?
You won't talk to them, will you?
We will learn English, won't we?
We will be happy, won't we?
You were here yesterday, weren't you?
We were at school, weren't we?

tr: Her şey çok güzeldi değil mi?
en: Everything was very beautiful, wasn't it?
Ayşe was a bit tired, wasn't she?
The party was very good, wasn't it?
You met with them, didn't you?
They didn't see us, did they?
You couldn't finish the school, could you?
You couldn't pay your rent, could you?
You didn't finish your homework, did you?
You gave the reports to your boss, didn't you?
Ali is meeting with us, isn't he?
Ali met with you, didn't he?
Ali will meet with you, won't he?
Ali had met with you, hadn't he?
You know English, don't you?

tr: Senin oğlun ingilizce konuşuyor değil mi?
en: Your son speaks English, doesn't he?
English is very easy, isn't it?

tr: Her gün 3 lt su içeceksin değil mi?
en: You will drink 3 liters of water every day, won't you?

tr: Senin biraz paran var değil mi?
en: You have some money, don't you?

tr: Ali'nin çok parası var değil mi?
en: Ali has a lot of money, doesn't he?

tr: Sizin bahçede çok kedi var değil mi?
en: There are many cats in your garden, aren't there?

tr: Türkiye'nin önemli problemleri var değil mi?
en: Turkey has important problems, doesn't it?

tr: Türkiye'de çok fazla Suriyeli var değil mi?
en: There are too many Syrians in Turkey, aren't there?
The water in your glass is cold, isn't it?

tr: Dünden beri ayşe'yi görmedin değil mi?
en: You haven't seen Ayşe since yesterday, have you?

tr: Sen 3 yıldır tatile gitmedin değil mi?
en: You haven't gone on a holiday for 3 years, have you?
You were born in 1978, weren't you?

tr: Onlar bizim kurallarımızı kabul edecek, değil mi?
en: They will accept our rules, won't they?

tr: Cep telefonunu restoranda unutmadın değil mi?
en: You didn't forget your mobile phone at the restaurant, did you?

tr: Cep telefonunu restoranda unuttun değil mi?
en: You forgot your mobile phone at the restaurant, didn't you?
You will always love me, won't you?

tr: Ben seni dün görmedim değil mi?
en: I didn't see you yesterday, did I?

tr: Ben seninle bugüne kadar tanışmadım, değil mi?
en: I haven't met you until today, have I?
We should learn English, shouldn't we?

tr: Daha iyi politikacılara sahip olabilirdik değil mi?
en: We could have better politicians, couldn't we?

tr: Çocuklarımız Daha iyi bir geleceğe sahip olabilirdi değil mi?
en: Our children could have a better future, couldn't they?

tr: Mutfakta çok yemek var değil mi?
en: There is a lot of food in the kitchen, isn't there?

tr: Buzdolabında hiç yemek yok değil mi?
en: There isn't any food in the fridge, is there?

tr: Senin çok arkadaşın var değil mi?
en: You have many friends, don't you?

tr: Ayşe'nin hiç arkadaşı yok değil mi?
en: Ayşe doesn't have any friends, does she?

tr: Yağmur yağdığında Ali okula gidiyordu, di mi?
en: Ali was going to school when it rained, wasn't he?
The children are running now, aren't they?
I am happy, aren't I?
